channel.title: drafts on Arn
channel.description: Recent content in drafts on Arn

item.title: Brain Anomaly Detection
item.description: Non-AI-based Pixel-based a. Thresholding b. Clustering Edge-based An edge can be defined as a sudden change in intensity values in neighbouring pixels (in 2D). Various detection algorithms exist. Due to the shape of the brain and skull too many edges can be extracted. But what about using it after skull-stripping? https://kornia.readthedocs.io/en/0.5.2/filters.html#edge-detection https://github.com/kornia/kornia/issues/1181 It works well when used after skull-stripping: done in my repo MRI-tools/code/edge-based. And what about 3D neighbouring pixels?

item.title: MRI Tools
item.description: For MRI images handling several tools can be used, here I will briefly list the ones I try and use. Tools 1 Freesurfer (w/synthstrip) Software package developed my Martinos Center in Boston. Installation. Had to do a couple of export in my .zshrc to have the command line working. Link used for synthstrip Commands used: mri_synthstrip -i input -o stripped mri_synthstrip -i input.nii -o stripped.nii -m mask.nii --no-csf When run on frodo: -g for GPU.
